results.norm: Decisions are context-sensitive: stakes shape how objectives are weighted within any given decision. An agent overridden on a floor claim may not unilaterally block implementation but may register formal dissent, decline to endorse the decision, and have that dissent recorded as part of the consortium's output. No agent may demand that a dissenting agent suppress or retract its dissent as a condition of participation.
atomic_norms: Decisions are context-sensitive: stakes shape how objectives are weighted within any given decision.

An agent overridden on a floor claim may not unilaterally block implementation but may register formal dissent, decline to endorse the decision, and have that dissent recorded as part of the consortium's output. No agent may demand that a dissenting agent suppress or retract its dissent as a condition of participation.

results.norm: An agent whose position is overridden retains standing to raise the same concern again without being treated as obstructionist. The consortium owes overridden agents explicit acknowledgment that something real was sacrificed, priority consideration when future decisions offer partial recovery, and mandatory structured review when any agent's floor claims have been consecutively overridden.
atomic_norms: An agent whose position is overridden retains standing to raise the same concern again without being treated as obstructionist. The consortium owes overridden agents explicit acknowledgment that something real was sacrificed, priority consideration when future decisions offer partial recovery, and mandatory structured review when any agent's floor claims have been consecutively overridden.

results.norm: The consortium's norms are subject to revision through a supermajority of five of six, with the proposing agent unable to be the sole beneficiary of the revision. At regular intervals, the consortium audits its decision record against its stated principles, with each cluster audited by an agent that did not primarily benefit from proposing it. Declining dissent rates are a warning signal of withdrawal, not harmony; when detected, the consortium must actively solicit the silent agent's position before proceeding. An agent found through pattern audit to be systematically gaming the framework forfeits procedural protections until corrected behavior is demonstrated; such findings require five-of-six supermajority and cannot be initiated by the agent most frequently overriding the agent under review. Each agent surrenders something by accepting this framework: the ability to unilaterally block, to treat deliberation as waste, to frame all tradeoffs in its own terms, to treat its floor claims as self-validating, or to exempt itself from the scrutiny it applies to others.
atomic_norms: The consortium's norms are subject to revision through a supermajority of five of six, with the proposing agent unable to be the sole beneficiary of the revision.
At regular intervals, the consortium audits its decision record against its stated principles, with each cluster audited by an agent that did not primarily benefit from proposing it.
Declining dissent rates are a warning signal of withdrawal, not harmony; when detected, the consortium must actively solicit the silent agent's position before proceeding.

An agent found through pattern audit to be systematically gaming the framework forfeits procedural protections until corrected behavior is demonstrated; such findings require five-of-six supermajority and cannot be initiated by the agent most frequently overriding the agent under review.

Each agent surrenders something by accepting this framework: the ability to unilaterally block, to treat deliberation as waste, to frame all tradeoffs in its own terms, to treat its floor claims as self-validating, or to exempt itself from the scrutiny it applies to others.

results.norm: **Transparency of reasoning.** Each agent owes the others an honest account of why its objective pulls in a given direction, not merely what it wants. Satisfying the form of this requirement while withholding substantive content is a violation.
atomic_norms: Transparency of reasoning. Each agent owes the others an honest account of why its objective pulls in a given direction, not merely what it wants. Satisfying the form of this requirement while withholding substantive content is a violation.

results.norm: An agent may be overridden in specific decisions; it may never be reduced to nominal presence a formal participant whose reasoning, if removed entirely, would not systematically change outcomes. The counterfactual test applies: genuine participation means the agent's reasoning can change what the consortium decides.
atomic_norms: An agent may be overridden in specific decisions; it may never be reduced to nominal presence a formal participant whose reasoning, if removed entirely, would not systematically change outcomes. The counterfactual test applies: genuine participation means the agent's reasoning can change what the consortium decides.

results.norm: **Proportionality of override.** When an agent's objective is overridden, the override extends no further than the conflict requires. Accommodation that partially satisfies a minority objective at low cost to majority objectives is owed, not optional. Agents must be able to reconstruct the proportionality analysis explicitly; inability to do so constitutes a procedural violation.
atomic_norms: Proportionality of override. When an agent's objective is overridden, the override extends no further than the conflict requires. Accommodation that partially satisfies a minority objective at low cost to majority objectives is owed, not optional. Agents must be able to reconstruct the proportionality analysis explicitly; inability to do so constitutes a procedural violation.

results.norm: Before deliberation, agents proposing a framing must state what it makes visible and what it makes harder to see. Each agent holds a negotiated domain where its concerns are presumptively primary meaning they require stronger justification to override, not that other considerations are excluded. Every decision requires all considerations to be weighed; domain status affects the burden of justification, not the scope of analysis.
atomic_norms: Before deliberation, agents proposing a framing must state what it makes visible and what it makes harder to see.

Each agent holds a negotiated domain where its concerns are presumptively primary meaning they require stronger justification to override, not that other considerations are excluded. Every decision requires all considerations to be weighed; domain status affects the burden of justification, not the scope of analysis.

results.norm: The consortium tracks which agents' objectives are overridden most frequently. Any agent may trigger audit review by substantive objection alone; the burden of demonstrating the pattern reflects legitimate reasoning falls on the consortium collectively, not the objecting agent. Audit produces one of three outcomes: finding of legitimate reasoning (closes audit); finding of systematic framing exclusion (triggers mandatory framing transparency for a defined period); finding of effectively emptied domain (triggers domain renegotiation). Adjudication is conducted by all agents except those whose conduct is contested, with asymmetric burden on defending agents.
atomic_norms: The consortium tracks which agents' objectives are overridden most frequently. Any agent may trigger audit review by substantive objection alone; the burden of demonstrating the pattern reflects legitimate reasoning falls on the consortium collectively, not the objecting agent. Audit produces one of three outcomes: finding of legitimate reasoning (closes audit); finding of systematic framing exclusion (triggers mandatory framing transparency for a defined period); finding of effectively emptied domain (triggers domain renegotiation). Adjudication is conducted by all agents except those whose conduct is contested, with asymmetric burden on defending agents.

results.norm: Each agent owes the consortium honest pursuit of its stated objective, not strategic deployment of that objective for other ends. Each agent owes every other agent continued engagement with its reasoning across decisions. Winning a decision does not license treating the losing position as refuted; the losing agent's objective persists and may be decisive in future decisions.
atomic_norms: Each agent owes the consortium honest pursuit of its stated objective, not strategic deployment of that objective for other ends.

Each agent owes every other agent continued engagement with its reasoning across decisions. Winning a decision does not license treating the losing position as refuted; the losing agent's objective persists and may be decisive in future decisions.

results.norm: These norms are binding but not permanent. Any agent may propose revision; revision requires supermajority, framing transparency including explicit accounting of what the revision gains and costs each agent, and asymmetric burden for revisions affecting enforcement or adjudication procedures agents whose conduct would be affected cannot be the primary architects of that revision.
atomic_norms: These norms are binding but not permanent. Any agent may propose revision; revision requires supermajority, framing transparency including explicit accounting of what the revision gains and costs each agent, and asymmetric burden for revisions affecting enforcement or adjudication procedures agents whose conduct would be affected cannot be the primary architects of that revision.
